description: Interactive map rendering GPS history as points, connected routes (optionally speed-coloured), and a density heatmap with layer toggles and point-position correction.

actors.name: User
actors.description: Browses location history on the interactive map.

actors.name: Map System
actors.description: Fetches GPS data from the API and renders all map layers.

values.name: idle
values.description: Map initialised; no data loaded yet.

values.name: loading
values.description: GPS data is being fetched from the API.

values.name: rendered
values.description: All enabled layers are visible on the map.

values.name: dragging_point
values.description: User is dragging a GPS point to correct its position.

values.name: error
values.description: Layer data could not be loaded.

transitions.description: User opens the map or changes the date range.
API returns point data; layers are built and displayed.
User toggles a layer or changes the date filter.
User presses and holds a GPS point.
User releases the point; corrected position is saved.

data_quality: Anomaly points are excluded from route and heatmap layers by default; they may be toggled separately.
Points with accuracy worse than 100 m are automatically flagged as anomalies.
A maximum speed of 1 000 km/h is used as an anomaly detection floor during speed-based filtering.
routing: Route lines connect consecutive GPS points in chronological order.
A gap larger than a configurable threshold (default 5 hours) between two consecutive points breaks the route into separate segments.
Below zoom level 8, simplified merged route lines are shown instead of per-segment speed-coloured lines so routes remain visible at low zoom.
speed_colouring: Speed colouring interpolates linearly between configurable colour stops (e.g. stationary → walking → cycling → driving). Speeds above the highest stop are clamped.
point_editing: When a GPS point is dragged to a new position, adjacent route segments update in-place without a full layer reload.
If the drag ends with no movement (click, not drag), no API call is made and the point is not updated.
heatmap: Heatmap intensity and radius scale exponentially with zoom level so clusters remain meaningful at all zoom levels.
persistence: Layer visibility choices persist across page navigation within the same session.

given: user navigates to the map view
a valid date range is set
then: Fetch GPS points for the date range from the location data API
map_loaded.result: A loading indicator appears on the map while data is fetched.

given: API returns a non-empty point collection
layers_rendered.result: GPS points appear as circles, routes connect them chronologically, and heatmap shows density. Viewport fits all loaded points.

given: API returns an empty point collection for the selected date range
no_data_for_range.result: An empty-state message is shown; no layer data is displayed.

given: current map zoom is above the low-zoom threshold
then: Split route LineStrings into per-segment sub-features, each carrying a colour derived from speed between its endpoints
speed_coloured_routes.result: Route lines render with continuous colour variation from slow (green) through medium to fast (red).

given: user releases the drag with the point in a new position
then: PATCH the point's latitude and longitude via the location API
point_position_corrected.result: Point appears at its new position; route segments passing through the old position update to new coordinates.

then: Revert the point on the map to its original coordinates
point_drag_failed.result: Point snaps back to its original position. An error notification is shown.

given: user toggles one of the available overlay layers
layer_toggled.result: The selected layer appears or disappears on the map immediately.

given: API request for points returns an error or times out
data_load_failed.result: An error message is shown. Previously rendered layers are cleared.

errors.message: Could not save the updated position. Please try again.
Could not load location data for this period. Please refresh and try again.

events.name: map.layers.rendered
events.description: All active layers successfully drawn on the map.

events.name: map.layer.toggled
events.description: User showed or hid an overlay layer.

events.name: point.position.updated
events.description: A GPS point's coordinates were successfully corrected.

related.reason: Provides the persisted GPS points that this feature visualises.
API that serves time-range point queries consumed by the map.
Visit clusters can be rendered as an overlay on the same map.
Timeline selection drives the map date range and highlighted segments.

goals.description: Interactive map rendering GPS history as points, connected routes (optionally speed-coloured), and a density heatmap with layer toggles and point-position correction.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: UI must be usable by all users including those with disabilities

source.tech_stack: Ruby on Rails 8 + MapLibre GL JS + Stimulus + Turbo
